id: frameworks/cyber kill chain/stage 6 command and control (c2)
title: Stage 6 Command and Control (C2)
relPath: Frameworks/Cyber Kill Chain/Stage 6 Command and Control (C2).md

breadcrumbs.id: frameworks/cyber kill chain/stage 6 command and control (c2)
breadcrumbs.title: Stage 6 Command and Control (C2)

overview: Following successful installation of persistence mechanisms, the attacker establishes a command and control (C2) channel. This covert communication line allows the attacker to issue commands, control compromised machines remotely, and begin orchestrating their broader objectives. The key goal here is reliable, stealthy, and flexible control.

study.overview: Following successful installation of persistence mechanisms, the attacker establishes a command and control (C2) channel. study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
